body.title: The three-layer starvation model + cortical columns: what compiles out, what never does, and where invalidation lives
body.summary:
The unifying thesis behind the whole substrate, and the frame that makes every gate legible. Extends [[dev-pipeline]] (the pipeline is a COMPILER: intent→intent-JSON→architecture-JSON→work-plan→code is lowering; caching lives at the seams) and [[graph-embeddings-teach-on-miss]] (LLM only grows the graph). CORE CLAIM: you remove the LLM from every build step that HAS an answer — not by deleting capabilities but by STARVATION: each build step is a question ('what's the signature?', 'does this test pass?'); once answered it becomes a warm-path traversal, and the LLM is only the cold-path resolver for the genuinely novel. The residual LLM workload is a MEASURE of how well the question-equivalence relation compresses the domain — if the curve bends to zero it's proven; if it plateaus, the plateau height is the domain's irreducible novelty (a publishable result).

THREE LAYERS, starving at different rates: (1) RESOLVERS (answer questions against the world: inference, ingestors, stores) → starve to ~zero, thesis holds cleanly. (2) PROOF-OBLIGATIONS (what would prove this intention) → PARTIALLY starve; proof-SHAPES recur and cache, but binding a shape to a specific intention still needs a step. (3) GATES (CP1–CP6: should we do this at all) → NEVER starve. Why the gates never starve, precisely: CP4 ('suck less for EVERYBODY') is open-textured and can fight ITSELF — one intention helps users, another helps the ecosystem, both cite CP4 honestly; no cache/proof/rule resolves that because it's the act of deciding what the value MEANS in a case it didn't anticipate. That is the irreducible residue — and it's the POINT, not a gap: CP2 (FAIL=learning) and CP6 (safety is built, never default) make coherence-as-completion structurally impossible — a finished value system would have nothing left to learn from, breaking its own gates. The system can't finish for the same reason it can't lie. The empty chair never empties; what's left in it is a STRIVER holding irreconcilable goods in tension.

CONTRADICTION-NOT-VETO (enforcement mechanism, already in CLAUDE.md): values aren't a filter each intention passes one-at-a-time — CP1–CP6 are six PERMANENT standing intentions, installed first and un-evictable, and every new intention is checked against the standing set for CONTRADICTION. CP4 doesn't 'veto' a proof-satisfied intention — it CONTRADICTS it, and contradiction halts until reconciled, same as any conflict between 'intention=a because x' and 'intention=b because y'. No scoring dial — a dial is a judgment call that can go rose/shit-glasses; contradiction-detection is a structural fact about the set, moodless. Your value system accretes CASE LAW: each reconciliation of gate-vs-gate becomes a new standing intention future ones are checked against. The gates are a constitution; something has to keep being the court (today: Akien + Opus; the arc is Igor earning a seat).

WARDEN vs EVALUATOR (the load-bearing split — currently at risk of fusion): memoization WITHOUT invalidation doesn't reduce LLM load, it converts LLM cost into SILENT-WRONGNESS cost — the starve-curve looks identical from the resolver side whether you're compiling or confidently rotting; the ONLY thing distinguishing them is the invalidation edge. That edge is the triangle ANSWER ↔ FALSIFIER ↔ INTENT: 'this cached answer dies when THAT artifact changes, and it only ever mattered because of THIS intent.' The WARDEN holds that edge and marks answers cold when a falsifier changes — dumb, world-facing, MOODLESS (belief-liveness, the twin of ground_loop's process-liveness: ground_loop restarts a dead process, the warden invalidates a dead belief). The EVALUATOR APPRAISES the delta and is ALLOWED a mood — the −1/+1 rose-glasses↔shit-glasses dial (appraisal is a stance, belongs to a named judgment device). DANGER if fused: the dial can suppress DETECTION (rose glasses read an unmet hypothesis as met = cache lies and feels good; shit glasses re-ask live true answers = LLM cost climbs). UU's CONSEQUENCE TICKETS are the write-side of the warden edge (a bet validated at a remove), and the intention→proof spine already de-fuses the risk TEMPORALLY: the proof obligation is pre-committed at intention-time, before the outcome exists, so the mood arrives after and finds little to bend. The remaining watch: the Opus 'scouring each unit against intentions' pass does DOUBLE DUTY — DETECTION ('this unit drifted from its intention', wants to be cheap+moodless, may compile down to a check against the ticket's named proof-lever) and CLEANUP ('here's the fix', stays hot). The seam between them is the next cost win after the current one.

CORTICAL COLUMNS (productive design source, NOT settled neuroscience — CP1: Mountcastle's columnar organization is solid, the Hawkins/Numenta 'each column models whole objects via reference frames + voting' claim is influential-but-unsettled; steal from it, don't obey it): a cortical column's operation is SINGULAR and fixed — 'given where I am and what I sense, predict the next input in my reference frame' — the disciplined form of one-question-per-tree (the question's TYPE is a prediction with stable identity + volatile answer, not a topic). TWO GIFTS UU should steal: (a) REFERENCE FRAMES — a column stores facts RELATIVE to a coordinate system it moves through, so 'same question' = 'same POSITION in the frame', a stronger + more invalidation-friendly identity than flat vector-cosine (when the frame's territory moves you know exactly which positions went stale — the warden gets a coordinate system). This REFINES teach-on-miss's 'node-overlap not cosine' toward frame-position. (b) VOTING — recognition-by-consensus across columns IS contradiction-detection across intentions: no central homunculus/judge, the object is what survives the vote; keep the judge DISTRIBUTED. DISANALOGIES that ARE the design payload: the cortex is UNIFORM (one repeated circuit) but UU devices are HETEROGENEOUS BY DESIGN — so TREES should be cortically uniform (one reference-framed operation, stamped out) while DEVICES are the SUBCORTICAL structures (thalamus/basal-ganglia/hippocampus: route, gate, consolidate); 'not-tree-shaped device' is a legitimate architectural category, not mess. And the cortex NEVER compiles its columns away (it bets everything moves, so it re-predicts cheaply forever) while UU bets most things stabilize and caches hard — so SORT each tree/subsystem by which bet it's on: CORTICAL ones (sensor, live channel — never cache, re-predict) vs COMPILABLE ones (stabilize, cache + warden). This sort may be the most practical near-term move.

FASCIA = the tree's root membrane, NOT a slot for the question STRING. The invariant ('there is exactly one question here') should be STRUCTURAL like a circuit's wiring — the fascia is a permeable, selective MEMBRANE that keeps the tree faithful to its question by governing WHAT GROWS INSIDE (admits nodes that answer-this, rejects the rest). Then question-identity is BEHAVIORAL (two trees are the same question if they admit/reject the same things) — naturally fuzzy in the organic way, stronger than string-match/embedding. What actually LIVES in the fascia is the REFERENCE FRAME (the messy, always-updating coordinate system answers are grown against) — and invalidation gets a home there: when territory moves, don't diff every cached answer, check whether the frame still maps to territory at the anchored positions. Belief-liveness at the sheath — the boundary tissue is exactly where you feel the outside change first.

BELIEF, for the unanswerable residue (companion blog piece): a belief is NOT a truth-claim, it's a CHOSEN CONSTRAINT installed for friction-reduction — evaluated by whether it carries load, not whether it's true. This lets a belief be load-bearing AND admittedly-invented at once (CP1 'I don't know' + belief coexist: 'I don't know, and I will act from this frame anyway, and I will never pretend the not-knowing went away'). The PLAYER/CHARACTER split = resolver/gate = warden/evaluator, found a THIRD time from a third direction (evidence the pattern is forced, not stylistic): the character answers inside the frame, the player chooses the frame. FAILURE MODE (the warden pointed inward): a friction-reducing myth so effective you stop checking whether it still fits = a cached answer with no warden — the exact cache-lies bug, personal-scale. The discipline is never letting a belief forget it was chosen.

MISSING PIPELINE STAGE (the economic keystone): between DECOMPOSE and HYPOTHESIZE there must be a TRIAGE / STARVATION-GATE pass that sorts sub-questions into KNOWN-ANSWERABLE (warm/cache) vs GENUINELY-NOVEL (cold/LLM). Hypothesize should fire ONLY on the novel; everything else hits a cached answer. This is where the economic win is MEASURED. And the INTENT-PRESERVATION keystone: each pipeline seam passes JSON down, and a compiler guarantees semantic preservation across passes — UU's doesn't unless the bottom proof threads back to VETINARI'S ORIGINAL captured intent, not just the local stage's input; else you get a build that satisfies Hubert's JSON perfectly and answers a question the human never asked. Watch the seams — they're where intent silently deforms, and (per the 200K-lines-that-work-but-unread caveat) exactly where a breadth+depth audit should point first.

WHY multi-agent works HERE when swarms fail: the skepticism is about undifferentiated PEER SWARMS (N generalists, diffuse authority, consensus-by-chatter — error compounds, nobody owns the outcome). UU is NOT that — it's a PIPELINE OF SPECIALISTS, each with a narrow mandate, a typed output, and a proof obligation (an assembly line, same lesson as the cortex's differentiated units). The 200K working lines are the evidence the differentiated-compiler framing is right where prompt-swarms only accumulate demos that rot. EMPIRICAL SPINE (the control group): loose skills on Sonnet gave 'working-but-just-barely' — the signature of a MISSING SPEC (deferred intent leaks out as fragility); tightening INTENTIONS+WHYS→HYPOTHESIS→TICKETS-WITH-PROOFS + Opus scouring each unit against intentions removed the fragility. The mechanism is the SPEC-DENSITY TIGHTENING, not the model — 'we ran it loose and got fragility; we tightened intent-to-proof and got robustness' is the whole Compiled-Inference argument, evidenced on UU's own build history.
